title: Arcane Trickster (Rogue Subclass)
excerpt: Enhance your roguish skills with a touch of arcane magic from the Wizard spell list, specializing in illusions and enchantments.
markdown:
# Arcane Trickster

**Enhance Stealth with Arcane Spells**

Some Rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with [[spells_chapter|spells]], learning magical tricks to aid them in their trade. Some Arcane Tricksters use their talents as pickpockets and burglars, while others are pranksters.

---
## Arcane Trickster Features

### Level 3: Spellcasting
You have learned to cast [[spells_chapter|spells]].

**Cantrips.** You know three [[cantrip|cantrips]]: [[mage-hand|Mage Hand]] and two other [[cantrip|cantrips]] of your choice from the [[wizard]] spell list.
Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one of your [[cantrip|cantrips]], except Mage Hand, with another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
When you reach Rogue level 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice.

**Spell Slots.** The Arcane Trickster Spellcasting table shows how many [[spell_level_slots|spell slots]] you have to cast your level 1+ [[spells_chapter|spells]]. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a [[long_rest]].

**Arcane Trickster Spellcasting**

| Rogue Level | Prepared Spells | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 3 | 3 | 2 | - | - | - |
| 4 | 4 | 3 | - | - | - |
| 5 | 4 | 3 | - | - | - |
| 6 | 4 | 3 | - | - | - |
| 7 | 5 | 4 | 2 | - | - |
| 8 | 6 | 4 | 2 | - | - |
| 9 | 6 | 4 | 2 | - | - |
| 10 | 7 | 4 | 3 | - | - |
| 11 | 8 | 4 | 3 | - | - |
| 12 | 8 | 4 | 3 | - | - |
| 13 | 9 | 4 | 3 | 2 | - |
| 14 | 10 | 4 | 3 | 2 | - |
| 15 | 10 | 4 | 3 | 2 | - |
| 16 | 11 | 4 | 3 | 3 | - |
| 17 | 11 | 4 | 3 | 3 | - |
| 18 | 11 | 4 | 3 | 3 | - |
| 19 | 12 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 1 |
| 20 | 13 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 1 |

**Prepared Spells of Level 1+.** You prepare the list of level 1+ [[spells_chapter|spells]] that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 Wizard [[spells_chapter|spells]].
The number of [[spells_chapter|spells]] on your list increases as you gain Rogue levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Arcane Trickster Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Wizard [[spells_chapter|spells]] until the number of [[spells_chapter|spells]] on your list matches the number in the table. The chosen [[spells_chapter|spells]] must be of a level for which you have [[spell_level_slots|spell slots]]. For example, if you're a level 7 Rogue, your list of prepared [[spells_chapter|spells]] can include five Wizard [[spells_chapter|spells]] of level 1 or 2 in any combination.

**Changing Your Prepared Spells.** Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one [[spells_chapter|spell]] on your list with another Wizard [[spells_chapter|spell]] for which you have [[spell_level_slots|spell slots]].

**Spellcasting Ability.** Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard [[spells_chapter|spells]].

**Spellcasting Focus.** You can use an Arcane Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Wizard [[spells_chapter|spells]].

### Level 3: Mage Hand Legerdemain
When you cast [[mage-hand|Mage Hand]], you can cast it as a [[bonus_action]], and you can make the spectral hand Invisible. You can control the hand as a [[bonus_action]], and through it, you can make Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks.

### Level 9: Magical Ambush
If you have the [[invisible_condition]] when you cast a [[spells_chapter|spell]] on a creature, it has [[disadvantage]] on any [[saving_throw]] it makes against the [[spells_chapter|spell]] on the same turn.

### Level 13: Versatile Trickster
You gain the ability to distract targets with your [[mage-hand|Mage Hand]]. When you use the Trip option of your Cunning Strike on a creature, you can also use that option on another creature within 5 feet of the spectral hand.

### Level 17: Spell Thief
You gain the ability to magically steal the knowledge of how to cast a [[spells_chapter|spell]] from another spellcaster.
Immediately after a creature casts a [[spells_chapter|spell]] that targets you or includes you in its area of effect, you can take a [[reaction]] to force the creature to make an Intelligence [[saving_throw]]. The DC equals your spell save DC. On a failed save, you negate the [[spells_chapter|spell's]] effect against you, and you steal the knowledge of the [[spells_chapter|spell]] if it is at least level 1 and of a level you can cast (it doesn't need to be a Wizard [[spells_chapter|spell]]). For the next 8 hours, you have the [[spells_chapter|spell]] prepared. The creature can't cast it until the 8 hours have passed.
Once you steal a [[spells_chapter|spell]] with this feature, you can't use this feature again until you finish a [[long_rest]].
